codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly tears down the live channel upon logout by utilizing Svelte's onDestroy hook in Shell.svelte to ensure the WebSocket is cleanly disconnected. It also properly mitigates bfcache back-navigation resurrection by adding a pageshow listener in App.svelte that reverifies session state. Comprehensive Playwright tests verify both logout invariants (VAL-CROSS-006) and user-switch state isolation (VAL-CROSS-007).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker proactively documented the frontend logout and user-switch flow, including architecture invariants and Playwright test coverage.

summary: Passed. The worker correctly implemented WebSocket teardown on logout via Svelte lifecycle hooks and added bfcache back-navigation protection. Extensive test coverage was added and all requirements were successfully met.
